item.title: Apple Debuts Business Service That Includes Offering Ads in Maps
item.description: Apple Inc. introduced a new platform that helps businesses manage devices and reach customers, in part by making it easier to advertise on its Maps app.

item.title: Microsoft to Rent Texas Data Center Dropped by Oracle, OpenAI
item.description: Microsoft Corp. has agreed to rent a data center project in Texas that was originally being developed for Oracle Corp. and OpenAI, according to people familiar with the situation.

item.title: SEC Questions Egan-Jones Over Bid to Again Rate Government Debt
item.description: Credit rating firm Egan-Jones Rating Co. wants to regain its ability to grade government debt and asset-backed securities after a ban more than a decade ago, but Wall Street’s top regulator isn’t so sure.

item.title: OpenAI’s Lightcap Sees Memory Shortage as Bottleneck Risk for AI
item.description: OpenAI Chief Operating Officer Brad Lightcap said that the ongoing memory chip shortage and constraints on US energy supplies are potential bottlenecks to expanding artificial intelligence infrastructure.

item.title: SNB Touts Record Number of New Bonds and Issuers on Swiss Market
item.description: The Swiss franc bond market set records in both new issuances and number of issuers last year, according to a senior central bank official.

item.title: Muni Deals Lure $1 Trillion Market With a Shift to Shorter Debt
item.description: A shift is afoot in how US municipal borrowers structure their debt sales.

item.title: United Airlines Warns of 20% Fare Hike to Cope With Oil Surge
item.description: United Airlines Holdings Inc. Chief Executive Officer Scott Kirby said ticket prices may have to go up by 20% if jet fuel prices remain elevated for longer.

item.title: West Africa to Feel Hit from War’s Fertilizer Shock, ETG Says
item.description: West Africa’s cocoa and cotton farmers will take the greatest hit as the war in Iran leads to a fertilizer shock across the continent, according to ETG, one of the region’s top agricultural commodity traders.

item.title: AI Cow Collar Startup Halter Raises $220 Million in Latest Deal
item.description: Halter, a New Zealand-based startup selling artificial intelligence-powered collars for cows, has raised $220 million from investors to expand the reach of its technology to more ranchers around the world.

item.title: Figure AI Founder's New Startup Hark Is Latest to Plan Family of AI Devices
item.description: Brett Adcock’s new startup Hark will be the latest to enter the burgeoning artificial intelligence devices field, with the serial founder saying the company is working on a family of products.

item.title: Finland Shelves Plan to Move Election Platform to Amazon Servers
item.description: Finland put on ice a plan to move its election platform to Amazon Web Services Inc., the latest development in Europe’s drive towards digital autonomy as trust in the US frays.

item.title: Angola Plans to Sell $2 Billion of Eurobonds as War Drives Crude
item.description: Angola plans to raise about $2 billion from a eurobond sale, according to people familiar with the matter, betting that higher crude prices driven by the Iran war will boost investor appetite for debt from the oil-rich nation.

item.title: Boralex Jumps as Analysts See Potential Deal at $6.3 Billion
item.description: Analysts covering Boralex Inc. say potential buyers may have to bid C$39 per share or more in a takeover, representing a premium of over 30% from where the stock was trading before Bloomberg News reported the firm is exploring a sale.

item.title: Kalshi Rides March Madness to New Record Despite NCAA Objections
item.description: The National Collegiate Athletic Association has pushed to shut down betting on college sports on prediction markets like Kalshi.

item.title: Russia Boosts Its Oil Revenues to Four-Year High as Prices Surge
item.description: Value of shipments hits most since just after Ukraine invasion, amid higher prices and flows

item.title: Fortnite Maker Epic Games Cuts About 1,000 Jobs Across Company
item.description: The company is seeing a downturn in engagement on Fortnite

item.title: Europe Pushes for Deal on FRTB Bank Capital Rules by Mid-June
item.description: The European Union is seeking to achieve a political deal within less than three months on shielding banks from the full force of new capital rules known as Fundamental Review of the Trading Book, amid concerns about eroding competitiveness.

item.title: IPL Team Royal Challengers Bengaluru Set for $1.8 Billion Takeover Deal
item.description: A consortium led by Indian billionaire Kumar Mangalam’s Aditya Birla Group, The Times of India Group, David Blitzer’s Bolt Ventures and Blackstone are set to buy the Royal Challengers Bengaluru, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Investors Are Betting on an Interest Rate Hike That’s Really Not That Likely
item.description: Investors have lifted odds that the Federal Reserve is on the verge of raising interest rates, but lingering labor-market fragility and the risks to growth from surging oil prices make that a long shot, at least for now.

item.title: Fund With Stakes in SpaceX and Anthropic Jumps 1,200% Above Asset Value
item.description: A newly-listed closed-end fund has soared more than 1,200% above its net asset value since its debut last week, as investors eager for stakes in IPO-bound companies such as SpaceX and Anthropic PBC push the vehicle’s market capitalization far beyond the estimated worth of its holdings.

item.title: Jim Ratcliffe’s Ineos Gets Debt Break From Iran War Supply Shock
item.description: Pressure on Ineos over its €15.5 billion ($18 billion) debt burden is easing as investors bet that the company’s earnings will benefit from disruptions to petrochemical supplies caused by the Iran war.

item.title: BOE Chief Economist Warns Against Delay in Tackling Price Surge
item.description: Bank of England Chief Economist Huw Pill warned rate-setters cannot allow “the fog of uncertainty” to delay action in tackling a resurgence in inflation caused by war in the Middle East.

item.title: Junk Bond Buyers Are Increasingly Refusing To Be ‘J. Crewed’
item.description: Credit markets coined the phrase to “pull a J. Crew” as shorthand for a borrower’s aggressive asset heist. Now junk bondholders are increasingly fighting back against such moves.

item.title: Norway Wealth Fund CEO Rules Out Job Cuts Despite AI Savings
item.description: Norway’s $2.1 trillion wealth fund is saving “billions of kroner” by using artificial intelligence in trading and investment decisions, but those gains will not lead to staff cuts, its chief executive officer said.

item.title: US Business Activity Growth Cools as Prices Rise on Iran War
item.description: Growth in US business activity slowed in March to an almost one-year low and prices paid for materials and other inputs picked up in the wake of the US-Israel war with Iran.

item.title: DeepSeek’s Latest Job Postings Highlight Pivot to Agentic AI
item.description: DeepSeek, the Chinese AI startup challenging the leaders of Silicon Valley, posted a cluster of job openings for specialists in agentic artificial intelligence and other positions, as enthusiasm for the technology that can carry out tasks without human intervention grips China.

item.title: FNB CEO Says Iran War May Derail South Africa’s Fragile Recovery
item.description: The conflict in the Middle East could derail South Africa’s best economic start in a decade as higher food and fuel prices rein in the fledgling recovery in the continent’s biggest economy, the head of one of the nation’s top retail lenders said.

item.title: Subprime Lender Goeasy Secures Debt Relief After Share Slide
item.description: Goeasy Ltd. won concessions from its lenders to keep key funding lines open after a surge in loan losses at its troubled auto lending unit sent shares and bonds tumbling, deepening scrutiny of the Canadian subprime lender’s financing model.

item.title: US Gas Market Sees Volatility With Shifting Weather and Oil Price Moves
item.description: US natural gas futures fluctuated between slight gains and losses as traders factored another bearish weather forecast against rising oil prices, which have pulled up the US gas contract since the war in Iran began.

item.title: Roblox’s Hit Game ‘Steal A Brainrot’ Battles Its Many Imitators
item.description: In the popular video game Steal A Brainrot, the object is petty theft. But now that the internet is awash with knockoffs of the runaway Roblox Corp. hit, its makers are defending their turf.
